Corporation Counsel l.. r. Ham
blen. F. T. Post and ]. M. Geraghty,
the committee Of attorneys on the
charter revision commission, ap-
pointed to prepare the various
amendments for submission to the
city council. have begun their
work and will have the amend
ments ready in time for presenta.
ton to the city council next Tues
day night


II the city council acts favorably,
and there does not seem to be
much question as to that, the
amendments will then be published
for 30 days in two daily news
papers. On the ballot at the city
election the amendments will be
referred to by section. with few
lines to indicate the character of
the amendment. For the full text
of the amendments the voter will
be called upon to read the official
publication in the newspapers.


The amendments to be voted up.
on are:


In crease OF power for the mayor,
giving him the right to employ and
dismiss the chief of police and
members of the board of public
works at pleasure.





Reorganization Of the board of
public works. making the city engi.
neer member.


RAnoval of the restriction as to
the amount of park bonds to be
issued In one year, making It i'm.
possible for the people to vote for
the talked Of 100000 bond issue
for parks.


Increase of the mayors salary
from $1,000 to $4,000 per year.


Making the police board consist
of the mayor and chief Of police.


Making the fire board consist OF
the mayor and chief of the fire
department


Election OF councilmen at large


Giving the city council the right
to create enlarged assessment
areas.


Assessing schools and county
property for public improvements


The question Of the nonpartisan
primary, the other important mat
ter agreed to by the commission
rests with the legislature.
